title: [Bug]: Oracle MySQL sink accepts blank inline passwords
summary: The Oracle MySQL sink accepted whitespace-only inline passwords.
observed: A blank inline password could pass validation and fail later as a database connection error.
expected: Blank inline passwords must be rejected during configuration validation.
reproduction: Run the focused Oracle MySQL hardening regression test for blank inline passwords.
impact: Operators receive a later destination failure instead of a precise configuration error.
delivery_semantics: The fix is startup-only validation and does not change write or ACK behavior.
security: Early rejection prevents ambiguous secret handling and avoids misleading runtime diagnostics.
acceptance: Oracle MySQL configuration rejects whitespace-only inline passwords.
Non-empty inline passwords remain accepted for local test use.
tests: Run the focused Oracle MySQL hardening regression test and the Oracle MySQL unit test suite.
documentation: Document that inline passwords must be non-empty and are intended for tests only.
closeout: Close only after the release containing the validation fix is published.
